steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's claim has two parts: people have (1) mixed feelings about cultured meat that (2) may be a barrier to adoption. The supporting quotation must show both — ambivalence toward cultured meat specifically, not just positive or negative views, and something that would actually block people from embracing it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'believed... good for the environment... few... willing to try eating it': shows consumers holding conflicting views — positive belief but behavioral reluctance — which is exactly the "mixed feelings" that would be a barrier to adoption. ✓
  • Choice B — 'less packaging... significant effect on the environment': concerns packaging for conventional meat, not cultured meat at all — irrelevant to the claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Advocates of lab-grown meat claim it's safer': presents only the positive side from advocates, not mixed feelings from consumers. ✗
  • Choice D — 'flavor of meat from livestock differs across species': describes variation in conventional livestock flavor — no connection to consumer attitudes about cultured meat. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
